cards.name: The Fool
cards.upright_keywords: New beginnings, adventure, freedom, carefree, innocence, intuition
cards.upright_meaning: The Fool represents new beginnings, adventure, and freedom. It symbolizes a carefree spirit and love for life. This card encourages you to embrace new opportunities, not fear the unknown, and trust your intuition.
cards.reversed_keywords: Recklessness, irresponsibility, naivety, foolishness, lack of direction
cards.reversed_meaning: When The Fool is reversed, it may indicate reckless or irresponsible behavior. You may be overly naive or lack direction, needing to consider your actions more carefully.
cards.symbolism: The Fool is typically depicted as a young man carrying a bundle, standing at the edge of a cliff with a dog beside him. This symbolizes fearlessness of the unknown and anticipation of new adventures.

cards.name: The Magician
cards.upright_keywords: Creativity, skill, willpower, confidence, planning, communication
cards.upright_meaning: The Magician represents creativity, skill, and willpower. It symbolizes your ability to transform ideas into reality. This card encourages you to believe in your abilities and use your skills to achieve your goals.
cards.reversed_keywords: Manipulation, deception, lack of direction, insufficient skill, hesitation
cards.reversed_meaning: When The Magician is reversed, it may indicate manipulation or deception. You may lack direction or have insufficient skills, needing more focus and effort.
cards.symbolism: The Magician is typically depicted holding a wand and elemental symbols, symbolizing his ability to manipulate various elements.

cards.name: The High Priestess
cards.upright_keywords: Intuition, subconscious, inner knowledge, mystery, insight, calm
cards.upright_meaning: The High Priestess represents intuition, subconscious, and inner knowledge. It symbolizes access to inner wisdom and understanding of mysteries. This card encourages you to trust your intuition and explore your inner world.
cards.reversed_keywords: Emotional confusion, ignored intuition, hidden enemies, blocked information
cards.reversed_meaning: When The High Priestess is reversed, it may indicate emotional confusion or ignored intuition. You may face hidden enemies or blocked information, needing to pay more attention to your inner voice.
cards.symbolism: The High Priestess is typically depicted as a woman sitting on a throne, holding a scroll, symbolizing her mastery of mystical knowledge.

cards.name: The Empress
cards.upright_keywords: Abundance, motherhood, creativity, relationships, art, nourishment
cards.upright_meaning: The Empress represents abundance, motherhood, and creativity. It symbolizes prosperity and nourishment. This card encourages you to embrace your creativity, nurture your relationships, and enjoy life's richness.
cards.reversed_keywords: Dependency, overindulgence, lack of confidence, blocked creativity
cards.reversed_meaning: When The Empress is reversed, it may indicate dependency or overindulgence. You may lack confidence or have blocked creativity, needing more independence and self-affirmation.
cards.symbolism: The Empress is typically depicted as a woman sitting on a throne surrounded by abundant nature, symbolizing her creativity and nurturing ability.

cards.name: The Emperor
cards.upright_keywords: Authority, structure, control, leadership, stability, protection
cards.upright_meaning: The Emperor represents authority, structure, and control. It symbolizes leadership and stability. This card encourages you to take responsibility, establish structure, and protect what you care about.
cards.reversed_keywords: Dictatorship, stubbornness, controlling behavior, lack of leadership, instability
cards.reversed_meaning: When The Emperor is reversed, it may indicate dictatorship or stubbornness. You may have excessive control issues or lack leadership, needing more flexibility and openness.
cards.symbolism: The Emperor is typically depicted as a man sitting on a throne, holding a scepter, symbolizing his authority and leadership.

cards.name: The Hierophant
cards.upright_keywords: Spiritual guidance, tradition, faith, education, morality, community
cards.upright_meaning: The Hierophant represents spiritual guidance, tradition, and faith. It symbolizes education and morality. This card encourages you to seek spiritual guidance, respect traditions, and engage with community.
cards.reversed_keywords: Dogmatism, blind faith, abuse of power, breaking tradition
cards.reversed_meaning: When The Hierophant is reversed, it may indicate dogmatism or blind faith. You may face abuse of power or need to break from tradition, requiring more critical thinking.
cards.symbolism: The Hierophant is typically depicted as a religious leader surrounded by followers, symbolizing his role as spiritual guide.

cards.name: The Lovers
cards.upright_keywords: Love, relationships, choice, harmony, partnership, attraction
cards.upright_meaning: The Lovers represents love, relationships, and choice. It symbolizes harmony and partnership. This card encourages you to make wise choices, nurture harmonious relationships, and enjoy the beauty of love.
cards.reversed_keywords: Relationship problems, difficulty choosing, disharmony, separation, temptation
cards.reversed_meaning: When The Lovers is reversed, it may indicate relationship problems or difficulty choosing. You may face disharmony or separation, needing to pay more attention to your relationships.
cards.symbolism: The Lovers is typically depicted as a couple witnessed by an angel, symbolizing love and choice.

cards.name: The Chariot
cards.upright_keywords: Victory, control, willpower, success, action, determination
cards.upright_meaning: The Chariot represents victory, control, and willpower. It symbolizes success and determination. This card encourages you to maintain control, stay focused on your goals, and take action to achieve your dreams.
cards.reversed_keywords: Conflict, lack of control, failure, frustration, hesitation
cards.reversed_meaning: When The Chariot is reversed, it may indicate conflict or lack of control. You may face failure or frustration, needing to concentrate your willpower.
cards.symbolism: The Chariot is typically depicted as a warrior driving a chariot, symbolizing his control and determination.

cards.name: Strength
cards.upright_keywords: Courage, strength, patience, control, compassion, inner strength
cards.upright_meaning: Strength represents courage, strength, and patience. It symbolizes control and compassion. This card encourages you to believe in your inner strength, remain patient, and solve problems with love and understanding.
cards.reversed_keywords: Weakness, lack of courage, loss of control, abuse, self-doubt
cards.reversed_meaning: When Strength is reversed, it may indicate weakness or lack of courage. You may face loss of control or self-doubt, needing to trust your inner strength more.
cards.symbolism: Strength is typically depicted as a woman gently controlling a lion, symbolizing her inner strength and courage.

cards.name: The Hermit
cards.upright_keywords: Solitude, introspection, wisdom, guidance, self-discovery, truth
cards.upright_meaning: The Hermit represents solitude, introspection, and wisdom. It symbolizes self-discovery and truth. This card encourages you to spend time alone, reflect on your life, and seek inner wisdom.
cards.reversed_keywords: Loneliness, isolation, lack of direction, refusing help, over-introspection
cards.reversed_meaning: When The Hermit is reversed, it may indicate loneliness or isolation. You may lack direction or be overly introspective, needing to be more open to receiving help.
cards.symbolism: The Hermit is typically depicted as an old man holding a lantern, walking alone on a path, symbolizing his introspection and wisdom.

cards.name: Wheel of Fortune
cards.upright_keywords: Destiny, change, cycles, opportunity, luck, turning point
cards.upright_meaning: Wheel of Fortune represents destiny, change, and cycles. It symbolizes opportunity and turning points. This card encourages you to accept life's changes, trust destiny's arrangements, and seize opportunities.
cards.reversed_keywords: Bad luck, stagnation, resistance to change, repeating patterns
cards.reversed_meaning: When Wheel of Fortune is reversed, it may indicate bad luck or stagnation. You may resist change or be stuck in repeating patterns, needing to be more open to life's changes.
cards.symbolism: Wheel of Fortune is typically depicted as a rotating wheel, symbolizing destiny's cycles and changes.

cards.name: Justice
cards.upright_keywords: Justice, balance, fairness, truth, law, responsibility
cards.upright_meaning: Justice represents justice, balance, and fairness. It symbolizes truth and responsibility. This card encourages you to seek justice, maintain balance, and take responsibility.
cards.reversed_keywords: Injustice, imbalance, prejudice, avoiding responsibility
cards.reversed_meaning: When Justice is reversed, it may indicate injustice or imbalance. You may face prejudice or be avoiding responsibility, needing to focus more on fairness and accountability.
cards.symbolism: Justice is typically depicted as a woman holding scales, symbolizing her fairness and justice.

cards.name: The Hanged Man
cards.upright_keywords: Sacrifice, waiting, perspective, pause, transformation, wisdom
cards.upright_meaning: The Hanged Man represents sacrifice, waiting, and perspective. It symbolizes transformation and wisdom. This card encourages you to see things from different angles, accept temporary sacrifices, and wait for the right moment.
cards.reversed_keywords: Stubbornness, excessive sacrifice, impatience, futility
cards.reversed_meaning: When The Hanged Man is reversed, it may indicate stubbornness or excessive sacrifice. You may lack patience or feel futile, needing to be more flexible and patient.
cards.symbolism: The Hanged Man is typically depicted as a man hanging upside down from a tree, symbolizing his different perspective and sacrifice.

cards.name: Death
cards.upright_keywords: Endings, transformation, rebirth, cleansing, new beginnings, metamorphosis
cards.upright_meaning: Death represents endings, transformation, and rebirth. It symbolizes cleansing and new beginnings. This card encourages you to accept endings, embrace transformation, and prepare for new beginnings.
cards.reversed_keywords: Resistance to change, fear of endings, stagnation, fear of death
cards.reversed_meaning: When Death is reversed, it may indicate resistance to change or fear of endings. You may face stagnation or fear of death, needing to be braver in accepting change.
cards.symbolism: Death is typically depicted as a skeleton riding a horse, symbolizing endings and transformation.

cards.name: Temperance
cards.upright_keywords: Balance, harmony, integration, moderation, patience, self-control
cards.upright_meaning: Temperance represents balance, harmony, and integration. It symbolizes moderation and self-control. This card encourages you to maintain balance, harmoniously integrate different elements, and practice self-control.
cards.reversed_keywords: Imbalance, excess, conflict, lack of control, extremism
cards.reversed_meaning: When Temperance is reversed, it may indicate imbalance or excess. You may face conflict or lack of control, needing to focus more on balance and moderation.
cards.symbolism: Temperance is typically depicted as an angel pouring liquid from one cup to another, symbolizing balance and integration.

cards.name: The Devil
cards.upright_keywords: Temptation, desire, bondage, materialism, addiction, shadow
cards.upright_meaning: The Devil represents temptation, desire, and bondage. It symbolizes materialism and shadow aspects. This card encourages you to recognize your temptations and desires, break free from bondage, and face your shadow self.
cards.reversed_keywords: Liberation, breaking free, overcoming temptation, self-control
cards.reversed_meaning: When The Devil is reversed, it may indicate liberation or breaking free. You may be overcoming temptation, practicing self-control, and freeing yourself from bondage.
cards.symbolism: The Devil is typically depicted as a demon with bound figures around him, symbolizing temptation and bondage.

cards.name: The Tower
cards.upright_keywords: Sudden change, chaos, collapse, awakening, liberation, revelation
cards.upright_meaning: The Tower represents sudden change, chaos, and collapse. It symbolizes awakening and revelation. This card encourages you to accept sudden changes, learn from them, and gain new insights.
cards.reversed_keywords: Resistance to change, delayed collapse, fear, limitation
cards.reversed_meaning: When The Tower is reversed, it may indicate resistance to change or delayed collapse. You may face fear or limitations, needing to be braver in facing change.
cards.symbolism: The Tower is typically depicted as a tower struck by lightning, symbolizing sudden change and collapse.

cards.name: The Star
cards.upright_keywords: Hope, inspiration, faith, peace, spirituality, healing
cards.upright_meaning: The Star represents hope, inspiration, and faith. It symbolizes peace and spirituality. This card encourages you to maintain hope, seek inspiration, and believe in life's beauty.
cards.reversed_keywords: Despair, lack of confidence, lost direction, spiritual depletion
cards.reversed_meaning: When The Star is reversed, it may indicate despair or lack of confidence. You may have lost direction or feel spiritually depleted, needing to reignite hope and faith.
cards.symbolism: The Star is typically depicted as a woman pouring water under a starry sky, symbolizing hope and inspiration.

cards.name: The Moon
cards.upright_keywords: Intuition, subconscious, dreams, illusion, emotions, mystery
cards.upright_meaning: The Moon represents intuition, subconscious, and dreams. It symbolizes emotions and mystery. This card encourages you to trust your intuition, explore your subconscious, and pay attention to your emotions.
cards.reversed_keywords: Confusion, fear, anxiety, deception, emotional instability
cards.reversed_meaning: When The Moon is reversed, it may indicate confusion or fear. You may face anxiety or deception, needing to pay more attention to your emotions and intuition.
cards.symbolism: The Moon is typically depicted as a full moon with dogs and wolves howling below, symbolizing the subconscious and emotions.

cards.name: The Sun
cards.upright_keywords: Success, joy, vitality, creativity, warmth, confidence
cards.upright_meaning: The Sun represents success, joy, and vitality. It symbolizes creativity and confidence. This card encourages you to enjoy success, stay joyful, and live life with vitality.
cards.reversed_keywords: Lack of vitality, depression, failure, self-doubt, unhappiness
cards.reversed_meaning: When The Sun is reversed, it may indicate lack of vitality or depression. You may face failure or self-doubt, needing to reignite your vitality and confidence.
cards.symbolism: The Sun is typically depicted as a bright sun with a child riding a horse below, symbolizing joy and vitality.

cards.name: Judgement
cards.upright_keywords: Judgement, rebirth, awakening, forgiveness, decision, calling
cards.upright_meaning: Judgement represents judgement, rebirth, and awakening. It symbolizes forgiveness and decision. This card encourages you to reflect on yourself, accept judgement, make wise decisions, and achieve rebirth.
cards.reversed_keywords: Guilt, self-criticism, resistance to change, delayed decision
cards.reversed_meaning: When Judgement is reversed, it may indicate guilt or self-criticism. You may resist change or delay decisions, needing to be more forgiving of yourself and make necessary decisions.
cards.symbolism: Judgement is typically depicted as an angel blowing a trumpet with people rising from graves, symbolizing judgement and rebirth.

cards.name: The World
cards.upright_keywords: Completion, success, fulfillment, integration, travel, achievement
cards.upright_meaning: The World represents completion, success, and fulfillment. It symbolizes integration and achievement. This card encourages you to celebrate your achievements, integrate your experiences, and enjoy life's completeness.
cards.reversed_keywords: Incompletion, failure, dissatisfaction, separation, stagnation
cards.reversed_meaning: When The World is reversed, it may indicate incompletion or failure. You may feel dissatisfied or stagnant, needing to work harder to complete your goals.
cards.symbolism: The World is typically depicted as a dancer surrounded by symbols of the four elements, symbolizing completion and integration.

cards.name: Ace of Wands
cards.upright_keywords: New beginnings, creativity, passion, adventure, inspiration
cards.upright_meaning: Ace of Wands represents new beginnings, creativity, and passion. It symbolizes adventure and inspiration. This card encourages you to start new projects, express your creativity, and pursue your goals with passion.
cards.reversed_keywords: Lack of direction, blocked creativity, fading passion, hesitation
cards.reversed_meaning: When Ace of Wands is reversed, it may indicate lack of direction or blocked creativity. You may feel your passion fading or hesitate to act, needing to rediscover your direction and inspiration.
cards.symbolism: Ace of Wands is typically depicted as a wand with new growth and flames, symbolizing new beginnings and creativity.

cards.name: Two of Wands
cards.upright_keywords: Planning, choice, balance, decision, vision
cards.upright_meaning: Two of Wands represents planning, choice, and balance. It symbolizes decision and vision. This card encourages you to make plans, make wise choices, and maintain balance.
cards.reversed_keywords: Indecision, lack of planning, imbalance, short-sightedness
cards.reversed_meaning: When Two of Wands is reversed, it may indicate indecision or lack of planning. You may face imbalance or be short-sighted, needing to clarify your goals and plans.
cards.symbolism: Two of Wands is typically depicted as a person holding two wands, standing high and looking into the distance, symbolizing planning and vision.

cards.name: Three of Wands
cards.upright_keywords: Vision, planning, expansion, adventure, business, success
cards.upright_meaning: Three of Wands represents vision, planning, and expansion. It symbolizes adventure and business success. This card encourages you to plan your future with vision, embrace expansion, and pursue business success.
cards.reversed_keywords: Lack of vision, poor planning, stagnation, failed adventure
cards.reversed_meaning: When Three of Wands is reversed, it may indicate lack of vision or poor planning. You may face stagnation or failed adventures, needing to plan your future more carefully.
cards.symbolism: Three of Wands is typically depicted as a person holding three wands, standing by the sea and looking into the distance, symbolizing vision and planning.

cards.name: Four of Wands
cards.upright_keywords: Celebration, success, stability, harmony, home, community
cards.upright_meaning: Four of Wands represents celebration, success, and stability. It symbolizes harmony and community. This card encourages you to celebrate your successes, build a stable foundation, and enjoy harmonious family and community life.
cards.reversed_keywords: Excessive celebration, instability, disharmony, family problems
cards.reversed_meaning: When Four of Wands is reversed, it may indicate excessive celebration or instability. You may face disharmony or family problems, needing to focus more on stability and harmony.
cards.symbolism: Four of Wands is typically depicted as an arch made of four wands with a celebration scene below, symbolizing celebration and success.

cards.name: Five of Wands
cards.upright_keywords: Conflict, competition, challenge, struggle, teamwork
cards.upright_meaning: Five of Wands represents conflict, competition, and challenge. It symbolizes struggle and teamwork. This card encourages you to face conflicts, compete positively, embrace challenges, and learn teamwork.
cards.reversed_keywords: Excessive conflict, failed competition, avoiding challenges, team disharmony
cards.reversed_meaning: When Five of Wands is reversed, it may indicate excessive conflict or failed competition. You may avoid challenges or face team disharmony, needing to manage conflicts more effectively.
cards.symbolism: Five of Wands is typically depicted as five people competing for wands, symbolizing conflict and competition.

cards.name: Six of Wands
cards.upright_keywords: Victory, success, recognition, confidence, leadership, honor
cards.upright_meaning: Six of Wands represents victory, success, and recognition. It symbolizes confidence and leadership. This card encourages you to celebrate your victories, enjoy the fruits of success, and demonstrate your leadership.
cards.reversed_keywords: Short-lived victory, limited success, lack of recognition, low confidence
cards.reversed_meaning: When Six of Wands is reversed, it may indicate short-lived victory or limited success. You may lack recognition or confidence, needing to work harder for lasting success.
cards.symbolism: Six of Wands is typically depicted as a victor riding a horse and holding a wand, symbolizing victory and success.

cards.name: Seven of Wands
cards.upright_keywords: Courage, defense, perseverance, challenge, opposition, confidence
cards.upright_meaning: Seven of Wands represents courage, defense, and perseverance. It symbolizes challenge and opposition. This card encourages you to bravely face challenges, stand your ground, and confidently defend your positions.
cards.reversed_keywords: Lack of courage, failed defense, giving up, low confidence
cards.reversed_meaning: When Seven of Wands is reversed, it may indicate lack of courage or failed defense. You may give up or lack confidence, needing to be braver in facing challenges.
cards.symbolism: Seven of Wands is typically depicted as a person holding a wand against six opponents, symbolizing courage and defense.

cards.name: Eight of Wands
cards.upright_keywords: Quick action, progress, news, travel, communication, momentum
cards.upright_meaning: Eight of Wands represents quick action, progress, and news. It symbolizes travel and communication. This card encourages you to act quickly, advance your goals, communicate actively, and seize travel opportunities.
cards.reversed_keywords: Slow action, blocked progress, delayed news, travel problems
cards.reversed_meaning: When Eight of Wands is reversed, it may indicate slow action or blocked progress. You may face delayed news or travel problems, needing to push things forward more actively.
cards.symbolism: Eight of Wands is typically depicted as eight wands flying through the air, symbolizing quick action and progress.

cards.name: Nine of Wands
cards.upright_keywords: Defense, vigilance, perseverance, resilience, preparation, patience
cards.upright_meaning: Nine of Wands represents defense, vigilance, and perseverance. It symbolizes resilience and preparation. This card encourages you to stay vigilant, persevere toward your goals, build resilience, and be well-prepared.
cards.reversed_keywords: Over-defense, excessive vigilance, exhaustion, lack of perseverance
cards.reversed_meaning: When Nine of Wands is reversed, it may indicate over-defense or excessive vigilance. You may feel exhausted or lack perseverance, needing to balance defense and offense.
cards.symbolism: Nine of Wands is typically depicted as a person holding a wand with wounds on their body, symbolizing defense and perseverance.

cards.name: Ten of Wands
cards.upright_keywords: Burden, responsibility, pressure, effort, achievement, challenge
cards.upright_meaning: Ten of Wands represents burden, responsibility, and pressure. It symbolizes effort and achievement. This card encourages you to take responsibility, face pressure, work hard, and ultimately achieve success.
cards.reversed_keywords: Overwhelming burden, avoiding responsibility, excessive pressure, limited achievement
cards.reversed_meaning: When Ten of Wands is reversed, it may indicate overwhelming burden or avoiding responsibility. You may face excessive pressure or limited achievement, needing to balance your responsibilities better.
cards.symbolism: Ten of Wands is typically depicted as a person carrying ten wands on their back, symbolizing burden and responsibility.

cards.name: Page of Wands
cards.upright_keywords: New ideas, creativity, enthusiasm, exploration, learning, adventure
cards.upright_meaning: Page of Wands represents new ideas, creativity, and enthusiasm. It symbolizes exploration and learning. This card encourages you to explore new ideas, express your creativity, stay enthusiastic, and keep learning.
cards.reversed_keywords: Lack of creativity, fading enthusiasm, blocked exploration, learning difficulties
cards.reversed_meaning: When Page of Wands is reversed, it may indicate lack of creativity or fading enthusiasm. You may face blocked exploration or learning difficulties, needing to reignite your enthusiasm and creativity.
cards.symbolism: Page of Wands is typically depicted as a young person holding a wand, symbolizing new ideas and enthusiasm.

cards.name: Knight of Wands
cards.upright_keywords: Action, adventure, enthusiasm, courage, speed, confidence
cards.upright_meaning: Knight of Wands represents action, adventure, and enthusiasm. It symbolizes courage and speed. This card encourages you to take action, embrace adventure, stay enthusiastic, and demonstrate courage.
cards.reversed_keywords: Impulsiveness, excessive adventure, uncontrolled enthusiasm, lack of planning
cards.reversed_meaning: When Knight of Wands is reversed, it may indicate impulsiveness or excessive adventure. You may face uncontrolled enthusiasm or lack of planning, needing to act more cautiously.
cards.symbolism: Knight of Wands is typically depicted as a knight on horseback holding a wand, symbolizing action and adventure.

cards.name: Queen of Wands
cards.upright_keywords: Enthusiasm, creativity, leadership, confidence, independence, charm
cards.upright_meaning: Queen of Wands represents enthusiasm, creativity, and leadership. It symbolizes confidence and independence. This card encourages you to show your enthusiasm, express your creativity, demonstrate leadership, and stay confident and independent.
cards.reversed_keywords: Excessive enthusiasm, blocked creativity, lack of leadership, low confidence
cards.reversed_meaning: When Queen of Wands is reversed, it may indicate excessive enthusiasm or blocked creativity. You may lack leadership or confidence, needing to balance your abilities better.
cards.symbolism: Queen of Wands is typically depicted as a woman holding a wand, symbolizing enthusiasm and leadership.

cards.name: King of Wands
cards.upright_keywords: Leadership, authority, creativity, confidence, success, enthusiasm
cards.upright_meaning: King of Wands represents leadership, authority, and creativity. It symbolizes confidence and success. This card encourages you to demonstrate your leadership, exercise your authority, express your creativity, stay confident, and pursue success.
cards.reversed_keywords: Lack of leadership, abuse of authority, blocked creativity, low confidence
cards.reversed_meaning: When King of Wands is reversed, it may indicate lack of leadership or abuse of authority. You may face blocked creativity or low confidence, needing to exercise power more balancedly.
cards.symbolism: King of Wands is typically depicted as a man holding a wand, symbolizing leadership and authority.

cards.name: Ace of Cups
cards.upright_keywords: New love, love, intuition, emotions, spiritual fulfillment
cards.upright_meaning: Ace of Cups represents new love, love, and intuition. It symbolizes emotions and spiritual fulfillment. This card encourages you to open your heart, embrace new love, and trust your intuition.
cards.reversed_keywords: Emotional blocks, love obstacles, ignored intuition, spiritual emptiness
cards.reversed_meaning: When Ace of Cups is reversed, it may indicate emotional blocks or love obstacles. You may ignore your intuition or feel spiritually empty, needing to pay more attention to your emotional needs.
cards.symbolism: Ace of Cups is typically depicted as a cup with liquid flowing out, symbolizing new love and affection.

cards.name: Two of Cups
cards.upright_keywords: Partnership, harmony, love, cooperation, balance
cards.upright_meaning: Two of Cups represents partnership, harmony, and love. It symbolizes cooperation and balance. This card encourages you to build harmonious relationships, cooperate with others, and maintain balance.
cards.reversed_keywords: Relationship problems, disharmony, conflict, imbalance
cards.reversed_meaning: When Two of Cups is reversed, it may indicate relationship problems or disharmony. You may face conflict or imbalance, needing to pay more attention to your relationships.
cards.symbolism: Two of Cups is typically depicted as two people exchanging cups, symbolizing partnership and harmony.

cards.name: Three of Cups
cards.upright_keywords: Celebration, friendship, joy, socializing, cooperation, sharing
cards.upright_meaning: Three of Cups represents celebration, friendship, and joy. It symbolizes socializing and cooperation. This card encourages you to celebrate your achievements, cherish your friendships, enjoy joy, socialize actively, cooperate and share with others.
cards.reversed_keywords: Excessive celebration, friendship problems, unhappiness, social isolation
cards.reversed_meaning: When Three of Cups is reversed, it may indicate excessive celebration or friendship problems. You may feel unhappy or socially isolated, needing to balance your social life better.
cards.symbolism: Three of Cups is typically depicted as three people toasting, symbolizing celebration and friendship.

cards.name: Four of Cups
cards.upright_keywords: Dissatisfaction, apathy, contemplation, choice, opportunity
cards.upright_meaning: Four of Cups represents dissatisfaction, apathy, and contemplation. It symbolizes choice and opportunity. This card encourages you to reflect on your dissatisfaction, re-evaluate your choices, and seize new opportunities.
cards.reversed_keywords: Excessive dissatisfaction, extreme apathy, missed opportunities, lack of reflection
cards.reversed_meaning: When Four of Cups is reversed, it may indicate excessive dissatisfaction or extreme apathy. You may miss opportunities or lack reflection, needing to face your emotions more positively.
cards.symbolism: Four of Cups is typically depicted as a person sitting under a tree with three cups before them and one cup in the sky, symbolizing dissatisfaction and choice.

cards.name: Five of Cups
cards.upright_keywords: Sorrow, disappointment, loss, regret, reflection, hope
cards.upright_meaning: Five of Cups represents sorrow, disappointment, and loss. It symbolizes regret and reflection. This card encourages you to face your sorrow, accept your losses, reflect on your experiences, and maintain hope.
cards.reversed_keywords: Excessive sorrow, overwhelming disappointment, inability to accept loss, lack of hope
cards.reversed_meaning: When Five of Cups is reversed, it may indicate excessive sorrow or overwhelming disappointment. You may be unable to accept loss or lack hope, needing to face your emotions more positively.
cards.symbolism: Five of Cups is typically depicted as a person standing before three fallen cups, symbolizing sorrow and loss.

cards.name: Six of Cups
cards.upright_keywords: Nostalgia, memories, friendship, gifts, generosity, harmony
cards.upright_meaning: Six of Cups represents nostalgia, memories, and friendship. It symbolizes gifts and generosity. This card encourages you to cherish your memories, maintain your friendships, give and receive gifts generously, and keep relationships harmonious.
cards.reversed_keywords: Excessive nostalgia, painful memories, friendship problems, over-generosity
cards.reversed_meaning: When Six of Cups is reversed, it may indicate excessive nostalgia or painful memories. You may face friendship problems or be overly generous, needing to balance your emotions better.
cards.symbolism: Six of Cups is typically depicted as one person giving a cup to another, symbolizing friendship and gifts.

cards.name: Seven of Cups
cards.upright_keywords: Dreams, choices, illusions, desires, creativity, possibilities
cards.upright_meaning: Seven of Cups represents dreams, choices, and illusions. It symbolizes desires and possibilities. This card encourages you to pursue your dreams, make wise choices, distinguish reality from illusion, and clarify your desires.
cards.reversed_keywords: Unrealistic dreams, difficulty choosing, illusion confusion, excessive desire
cards.reversed_meaning: When Seven of Cups is reversed, it may indicate unrealistic dreams or difficulty choosing. You may be confused by illusions or have excessive desires, needing to face your dreams and choices more realistically.
cards.symbolism: Seven of Cups is typically depicted as a person before seven cups, each containing different illusions, symbolizing dreams and choices.

cards.name: Eight of Cups
cards.upright_keywords: Leaving, seeking, growth, transformation, new beginnings, exploration
cards.upright_meaning: Eight of Cups represents leaving, seeking, and growth. It symbolizes transformation and new beginnings. This card encourages you to leave unsatisfying situations, seek new opportunities, pursue growth, embrace transformation, and start new journeys.
cards.reversed_keywords: Unwillingness to leave, failed search, blocked growth, difficult transformation
cards.reversed_meaning: When Eight of Cups is reversed, it may indicate unwillingness to leave or failed search. You may face blocked growth or difficult transformation, needing to face change more bravely.
cards.symbolism: Eight of Cups is typically depicted as a person leaving eight cups, symbolizing leaving and seeking.

cards.name: Nine of Cups
cards.upright_keywords: Contentment, happiness, success, emotional abundance, wish fulfillment
cards.upright_meaning: Nine of Cups represents contentment, happiness, and success. It symbolizes emotional abundance and wish fulfillment. This card encourages you to enjoy your contentment, feel your happiness, celebrate your success, and enrich your emotional life.
cards.reversed_keywords: Excessive contentment, fleeting happiness, limited success, emotional emptiness
cards.reversed_meaning: When Nine of Cups is reversed, it may indicate excessive contentment or fleeting happiness. You may face limited success or emotional emptiness, needing to balance your emotions better.
cards.symbolism: Nine of Cups is typically depicted as a person sitting before nine cups, symbolizing contentment and happiness.

cards.name: Ten of Cups
cards.upright_keywords: Family, harmony, happiness, contentment, love, reunion
cards.upright_meaning: Ten of Cups represents family, harmony, and happiness. It symbolizes contentment and love. This card encourages you to cherish your family, maintain harmonious relationships, enjoy happy life, and feel the warmth of love.
cards.reversed_keywords: Family problems, disharmony, unhappiness, lack of contentment
cards.reversed_meaning: When Ten of Cups is reversed, it may indicate family problems or disharmony. You may feel unhappy or lack contentment, needing to pay more attention to your family and relationships.
cards.symbolism: Ten of Cups is typically depicted as a family standing before ten cups, symbolizing family and happiness.

cards.name: Page of Cups
cards.upright_keywords: Emotions, intuition, creativity, dreams, learning, sensitivity
cards.upright_meaning: Page of Cups represents emotions, intuition, and creativity. It symbolizes dreams and learning. This card encourages you to pay attention to your emotions, trust your intuition, express your creativity, pursue your dreams, and keep learning.
cards.reversed_keywords: Emotional confusion, ignored intuition, blocked creativity, unrealistic dreams
cards.reversed_meaning: When Page of Cups is reversed, it may indicate emotional confusion or ignored intuition. You may face blocked creativity or unrealistic dreams, needing to balance your emotions and creativity better.
cards.symbolism: Page of Cups is typically depicted as a young person holding a cup, symbolizing emotions and intuition.

cards.name: Knight of Cups
cards.upright_keywords: Romance, emotions, intuition, creativity, tenderness, dreams
cards.upright_meaning: Knight of Cups represents romance, emotions, and intuition. It symbolizes creativity and tenderness. This card encourages you to show your romance, express your emotions, trust your intuition, express your creativity, and stay tender.
cards.reversed_keywords: Excessive romance, emotional confusion, ignored intuition, blocked creativity
cards.reversed_meaning: When Knight of Cups is reversed, it may indicate excessive romance or emotional confusion. You may ignore your intuition or have blocked creativity, needing to balance your emotions and creativity better.
cards.symbolism: Knight of Cups is typically depicted as a knight on horseback holding a cup, symbolizing romance and emotions.

cards.name: Queen of Cups
cards.upright_keywords: Emotions, intuition, tenderness, creativity, insight, empathy
cards.upright_meaning: Queen of Cups represents emotions, intuition, and tenderness. It symbolizes creativity and empathy. This card encourages you to pay attention to your emotions, trust your intuition, stay tender, express your creativity, and show your empathy.
cards.reversed_keywords: Excessive emotion, ignored intuition, lack of tenderness, blocked creativity
cards.reversed_meaning: When Queen of Cups is reversed, it may indicate excessive emotion or ignored intuition. You may lack tenderness or have blocked creativity, needing to balance your emotions and intuition better.
cards.symbolism: Queen of Cups is typically depicted as a woman holding a cup, symbolizing emotions and intuition.

cards.name: King of Cups
cards.upright_keywords: Emotional balance, intuition, wisdom, leadership, creativity, empathy
cards.upright_meaning: King of Cups represents emotional balance, intuition, and wisdom. It symbolizes leadership and empathy. This card encourages you to maintain emotional balance, trust your intuition, use your wisdom, demonstrate your leadership, express your creativity, and show your empathy.
cards.reversed_keywords: Emotional imbalance, ignored intuition, lack of wisdom, poor leadership
cards.reversed_meaning: When King of Cups is reversed, it may indicate emotional imbalance or ignored intuition. You may lack wisdom or leadership ability, needing to balance your emotions and intuition better.
cards.symbolism: King of Cups is typically depicted as a man holding a cup, symbolizing emotional balance and wisdom.

cards.name: Ace of Swords
cards.upright_keywords: Truth, clarity, wisdom, breakthrough, new ideas
cards.upright_meaning: Ace of Swords represents truth, clarity, and wisdom. It symbolizes breakthrough and new ideas. This card encourages you to seek truth, maintain clear thinking, break through obstacles, and generate new ideas.
cards.reversed_keywords: Confusion, chaos, deception, mental blocks, misunderstanding
cards.reversed_meaning: When Ace of Swords is reversed, it may indicate confusion or chaos. You may face deception or mental blocks, needing to think more clearly and seek truth.
cards.symbolism: Ace of Swords is typically depicted as a sword piercing through clouds, symbolizing truth and breakthrough.

cards.name: Two of Swords
cards.upright_keywords: Balance, peace, neutrality, choice, harmony
cards.upright_meaning: Two of Swords represents balance, peace, and neutrality. It symbolizes choice and harmony. This card encourages you to maintain balance, seek peace, make wise choices, and keep harmony.
cards.reversed_keywords: Conflict, imbalance, extremism, indecision, disharmony
cards.reversed_meaning: When Two of Swords is reversed, it may indicate conflict or imbalance. You may face extremism or indecision, needing to strive harder for balance and harmony.
cards.symbolism: Two of Swords is typically depicted as a person holding two swords with blindfold, symbolizing balance and neutrality.

cards.name: Three of Swords
cards.upright_keywords: Sorrow, pain, heartbreak, conflict, betrayal, loss
cards.upright_meaning: Three of Swords represents sorrow, pain, and heartbreak. It symbolizes conflict and betrayal. This card encourages you to face your sorrow, accept your pain, deal with your heartbreak, resolve conflicts, and recover from betrayal.
cards.reversed_keywords: Excessive sorrow, intensified pain, escalating conflict, inability to recover
cards.reversed_meaning: When Three of Swords is reversed, it may indicate excessive sorrow or intensified pain. You may face escalating conflict or inability to recover, needing to face your emotions more positively.
cards.symbolism: Three of Swords is typically depicted as three swords piercing a heart, symbolizing sorrow and pain.

cards.name: Four of Swords
cards.upright_keywords: Rest, meditation, recovery, reflection, calm, focus
cards.upright_meaning: Four of Swords represents rest, meditation, and recovery. It symbolizes reflection and calm. This card encourages you to rest your body and mind, practice meditation, recover your energy, reflect on your experiences, and stay calm and focused.
cards.reversed_keywords: Excessive rest, blocked meditation, slow recovery, lack of reflection
cards.reversed_meaning: When Four of Swords is reversed, it may indicate excessive rest or blocked meditation. You may face slow recovery or lack of reflection, needing to balance rest and activity better.
cards.symbolism: Four of Swords is typically depicted as a person resting under three swords, symbolizing rest and recovery.

cards.name: Five of Swords
cards.upright_keywords: Conflict, competition, victory, loss, selfishness, disharmony
cards.upright_meaning: Five of Swords represents conflict, competition, and victory. It symbolizes loss and selfishness. This card encourages you to handle conflicts, engage in healthy competition, accept victory and loss, avoid selfishness, and seek harmony.
cards.reversed_keywords: Escalating conflict, excessive competition, empty victory, intensified loss
cards.reversed_meaning: When Five of Swords is reversed, it may indicate escalating conflict or excessive competition. You may face empty victory or intensified loss, needing to balance conflict and competition better.
cards.symbolism: Five of Swords is typically depicted as a person holding five swords, symbolizing conflict and victory.

cards.name: Six of Swords
cards.upright_keywords: Transition, travel, peace, recovery, hope, moving forward
cards.upright_meaning: Six of Swords represents transition, travel, and peace. It symbolizes recovery and hope. This card encourages you to accept transition, travel, seek peace, recover your energy, maintain hope, and keep moving forward.
cards.reversed_keywords: Difficult transition, travel problems, blocked peace, slow recovery
cards.reversed_meaning: When Six of Swords is reversed, it may indicate difficult transition or travel problems. You may face blocked peace or slow recovery, needing to face transition and recovery more positively.
cards.symbolism: Six of Swords is typically depicted as a person rowing a boat with six swords, symbolizing transition and travel.

cards.name: Seven of Swords
cards.upright_keywords: Strategy, deception, secrets, planning, wisdom, caution
cards.upright_meaning: Seven of Swords represents strategy, deception, and secrets. It symbolizes planning and wisdom. This card encourages you to use strategy, avoid deception, keep secrets, make plans, use your wisdom, and stay cautious.
cards.reversed_keywords: Failed strategy, exposed deception, leaked secrets, blocked plans
cards.reversed_meaning: When Seven of Swords is reversed, it may indicate failed strategy or exposed deception. You may face leaked secrets or blocked plans, needing to handle your affairs more honestly and cautiously.
cards.symbolism: Seven of Swords is typically depicted as a person stealing seven swords, symbolizing strategy and secrets.

cards.name: Eight of Swords
cards.upright_keywords: Limitation, dilemma, fear, bondage, self-imposed restriction, confusion
cards.upright_meaning: Eight of Swords represents limitation, dilemma, and fear. It symbolizes bondage and self-imposed restriction. This card encourages you to identify your limitations, face your dilemmas, overcome your fears, break free from bondage, break through self-imposed restrictions, and eliminate confusion.
cards.reversed_keywords: Intensified limitation, deepened dilemma, increased fear, strengthened bondage
cards.reversed_meaning: When Eight of Swords is reversed, it may indicate intensified limitation or deepened dilemma. You may face increased fear or strengthened bondage, needing to break through your limitations more actively.
cards.symbolism: Eight of Swords is typically depicted as a person surrounded by eight swords, symbolizing limitation and bondage.

cards.name: Nine of Swords
cards.upright_keywords: Anxiety, fear, nightmares, worry, stress, insomnia
cards.upright_meaning: Nine of Swords represents anxiety, fear, and nightmares. It symbolizes worry and stress. This card encourages you to face your anxiety, overcome your fears, break free from nightmares, reduce your worries, relieve your stress, and improve your sleep.
cards.reversed_keywords: Intensified anxiety, increased fear, frequent nightmares, excessive worry
cards.reversed_meaning: When Nine of Swords is reversed, it may indicate intensified anxiety or increased fear. You may face frequent nightmares or excessive worry, needing to handle your emotions and stress more actively.
cards.symbolism: Nine of Swords is typically depicted as a person in bed with nine swords hanging above, symbolizing anxiety and fear.

cards.name: Ten of Swords
cards.upright_keywords: Endings, pain, loss, transformation, new beginnings, release
cards.upright_meaning: Ten of Swords represents endings, pain, and loss. It symbolizes transformation and new beginnings. This card encourages you to accept endings, face pain, deal with loss, embrace transformation, prepare for new beginnings, and release the past.
cards.reversed_keywords: Difficult endings, intensified pain, expanded loss, blocked transformation
cards.reversed_meaning: When Ten of Swords is reversed, it may indicate difficult endings or intensified pain. You may face expanded loss or blocked transformation, needing to accept endings and transformation more bravely.
cards.symbolism: Ten of Swords is typically depicted as a person pierced by ten swords, symbolizing endings and pain.

cards.name: Page of Swords
cards.upright_keywords: Curiosity, learning, communication, information, thinking, exploration
cards.upright_meaning: Page of Swords represents curiosity, learning, and communication. It symbolizes information and thinking. This card encourages you to stay curious, keep learning, communicate actively, seek information, think clearly, and explore new areas.
cards.reversed_keywords: Excessive curiosity, learning difficulties, poor communication, information overload
cards.reversed_meaning: When Page of Swords is reversed, it may indicate excessive curiosity or learning difficulties. You may face poor communication or information overload, needing to balance your learning and communication better.
cards.symbolism: Page of Swords is typically depicted as a young person holding a sword, symbolizing curiosity and learning.

cards.name: Knight of Swords
cards.upright_keywords: Action, thinking, communication, speed, courage, decisiveness
cards.upright_meaning: Knight of Swords represents action, thinking, and communication. It symbolizes speed and courage. This card encourages you to take action, think clearly, communicate actively, make quick decisions, demonstrate courage, and be decisive.
cards.reversed_keywords: Impulsiveness, poor thinking, poor communication, recklessness, indecision
cards.reversed_meaning: When Knight of Swords is reversed, it may indicate impulsiveness or poor thinking. You may face poor communication or recklessness, needing to think more clearly before acting.
cards.symbolism: Knight of Swords is typically depicted as a knight on horseback holding a sword, symbolizing action and thinking.

cards.name: Queen of Swords
cards.upright_keywords: Intellect, clarity, independence, communication, insight, objectivity
cards.upright_meaning: Queen of Swords represents intellect, clarity, and independence. It symbolizes communication and insight. This card encourages you to use your intellect, maintain clarity, stay independent, communicate effectively, gain insight, and remain objective.
cards.reversed_keywords: Intellectual rigidity, lack of clarity, dependence, poor communication, lack of insight
cards.reversed_meaning: When Queen of Swords is reversed, it may indicate intellectual rigidity or lack of clarity. You may face dependence or poor communication, needing to balance intellect and emotion better.
cards.symbolism: Queen of Swords is typically depicted as a woman holding a sword, symbolizing intellect and clarity.

cards.name: King of Swords
cards.upright_keywords: Intellect, logic, leadership, authority, clarity, justice
cards.upright_meaning: King of Swords represents intellect, logic, and leadership. It symbolizes authority and clarity. This card encourages you to use your intellect, think logically, demonstrate leadership, exercise authority, maintain clarity, and uphold justice.
cards.reversed_keywords: Intellectual arrogance, flawed logic, poor leadership, abuse of authority, lack of clarity
cards.reversed_meaning: When King of Swords is reversed, it may indicate intellectual arrogance or flawed logic. You may face poor leadership or abuse of authority, needing to use power more responsibly.
cards.symbolism: King of Swords is typically depicted as a man holding a sword, symbolizing intellect and authority.

cards.name: Ace of Pentacles
cards.upright_keywords: New opportunity, wealth, abundance, security, manifestation
cards.upright_meaning: Ace of Pentacles represents new opportunity, wealth, and abundance. It symbolizes security and manifestation. This card encourages you to seize new opportunities, build wealth, embrace abundance, establish security, and manifest your goals.
cards.reversed_keywords: Missed opportunity, financial problems, lack of abundance, insecurity
cards.reversed_meaning: When Ace of Pentacles is reversed, it may indicate missed opportunities or financial problems. You may lack abundance or feel insecure, needing to be more proactive in seeking opportunities.
cards.symbolism: Ace of Pentacles is typically depicted as a pentacle with green growth, symbolizing new opportunity and abundance.

cards.name: Two of Pentacles
cards.upright_keywords: Balance, adaptability, juggling, harmony, flexibility
cards.upright_meaning: Two of Pentacles represents balance, adaptability, and juggling. It symbolizes harmony and flexibility. This card encourages you to maintain balance, adapt to changes, handle multiple responsibilities, keep harmony, and stay flexible.
cards.reversed_keywords: Imbalance, inflexibility, overwhelmed, disharmony
cards.reversed_meaning: When Two of Pentacles is reversed, it may indicate imbalance or inflexibility. You may feel overwhelmed or face disharmony, needing to balance your responsibilities better.
cards.symbolism: Two of Pentacles is typically depicted as a person juggling two pentacles, symbolizing balance and adaptability.

cards.name: Three of Pentacles
cards.upright_keywords: Teamwork, collaboration, skill, recognition, craftsmanship
cards.upright_meaning: Three of Pentacles represents teamwork, collaboration, and skill. It symbolizes recognition and craftsmanship. This card encourages you to work in teams, collaborate with others, develop your skills, seek recognition, and pursue excellence.
cards.reversed_keywords: Poor teamwork, lack of collaboration, skill deficits, lack of recognition
cards.reversed_meaning: When Three of Pentacles is reversed, it may indicate poor teamwork or lack of collaboration. You may face skill deficits or lack of recognition, needing to improve your teamwork and skills.
cards.symbolism: Three of Pentacles is typically depicted as three people examining a pentacle, symbolizing teamwork and craftsmanship.

cards.name: Four of Pentacles
cards.upright_keywords: Security, stability, possession, conservatism, control
cards.upright_meaning: Four of Pentacles represents security, stability, and possession. It symbolizes conservatism and control. This card encourages you to establish security, maintain stability, manage your possessions wisely, be conservative when needed, and exercise control.
cards.reversed_keywords: Insecurity, instability, greed, over-possessiveness, loss of control
cards.reversed_meaning: When Four of Pentacles is reversed, it may indicate insecurity or instability. You may face greed or over-possessiveness, needing to balance your relationship with material things.
cards.symbolism: Four of Pentacles is typically depicted as a person holding four pentacles, symbolizing security and possession.

cards.name: Five of Pentacles
cards.upright_keywords: Financial difficulty, loss, poverty, isolation, hardship
cards.upright_meaning: Five of Pentacles represents financial difficulty, loss, and poverty. It symbolizes isolation and hardship. This card encourages you to face financial difficulties bravely, accept losses, seek help when needed, overcome isolation, and persevere through hardships.
cards.reversed_keywords: Financial recovery, overcoming loss, ending poverty, reconnecting
cards.reversed_meaning: When Five of Pentacles is reversed, it may indicate financial recovery or overcoming loss. You may be ending poverty or reconnecting with others, moving toward better times.
cards.symbolism: Five of Pentacles is typically depicted as two beggars in the snow, symbolizing hardship and poverty.

cards.name: Six of Pentacles
cards.upright_keywords: Generosity, charity, giving, receiving, balance, fairness
cards.upright_meaning: Six of Pentacles represents generosity, charity, and giving. It symbolizes receiving and balance. This card encourages you to be generous, practice charity, give and receive graciously, maintain balance, and uphold fairness.
cards.reversed_keywords: Selfishness, stinginess, unfair giving, imbalance
cards.reversed_meaning: When Six of Pentacles is reversed, it may indicate selfishness or stinginess. You may face unfair giving or imbalance, needing to be more generous and fair.
cards.symbolism: Six of Pentacles is typically depicted as a person giving pentacles to others, symbolizing generosity and charity.

cards.name: Seven of Pentacles
cards.upright_keywords: Patience, investment, return, growth, planning, evaluation
cards.upright_meaning: Seven of Pentacles represents patience, investment, and return. It symbolizes growth and planning. This card encourages you to maintain patience, make wise investments, expect returns, focus on growth, make plans, and evaluate your progress.
cards.reversed_keywords: Impatience, failed investment, delayed return, blocked growth
cards.reversed_meaning: When Seven of Pentacles is reversed, it may indicate impatience or failed investment. You may face delayed returns or blocked growth, needing to wait more patiently and evaluate your investments.
cards.symbolism: Seven of Pentacles is typically depicted as a person examining his plants, symbolizing patience and investment.

cards.name: Eight of Pentacles
cards.upright_keywords: Effort, skill, focus, craftsmanship, learning, progress
cards.upright_meaning: Eight of Pentacles represents effort, skill, and focus. It symbolizes craftsmanship and learning. This card encourages you to work hard, develop your skills, stay focused, refine your craftsmanship, keep learning, and pursue progress.
cards.reversed_keywords: Insufficient effort, lack of skill, lack of focus, poor craftsmanship
cards.reversed_meaning: When Eight of Pentacles is reversed, it may indicate insufficient effort or lack of skill. You may lack focus or have poor craftsmanship, needing to work harder and learn more.
cards.symbolism: Eight of Pentacles is typically depicted as a person making pentacles, symbolizing effort and craftsmanship.

cards.name: Nine of Pentacles
cards.upright_keywords: Success, abundance, independence, enjoyment, confidence, comfort
cards.upright_meaning: Nine of Pentacles represents success, abundance, and independence. It symbolizes enjoyment and confidence. This card encourages you to enjoy your success, feel abundance, stay independent, enjoy life's comforts, and maintain confidence.
cards.reversed_keywords: Short-lived success, insufficient abundance, dependence, over-indulgence
cards.reversed_meaning: When Nine of Pentacles is reversed, it may indicate short-lived success or insufficient abundance. You may face dependence or over-indulgence, needing to be more independent and balance your enjoyment of success.
cards.symbolism: Nine of Pentacles is typically depicted as a person enjoying her wealth in a garden, symbolizing success and abundance.

cards.name: Ten of Pentacles
cards.upright_keywords: Wealth, family, inheritance, security, success, stability
cards.upright_meaning: Ten of Pentacles represents wealth, family, and inheritance. It symbolizes security and success. This card encourages you to accumulate wealth, focus on family, value inheritance, establish security, celebrate success, and maintain stability.
cards.reversed_keywords: Wealth loss, family problems, inheritance disputes, lack of security
cards.reversed_meaning: When Ten of Pentacles is reversed, it may indicate wealth loss or family problems. You may face inheritance disputes or lack of security, needing to focus more on your family and financial security.
cards.symbolism: Ten of Pentacles is typically depicted as a family standing before ten pentacles, symbolizing wealth and family.

cards.name: Page of Pentacles
cards.upright_keywords: Learning, skill, practicality, patience, finance, responsibility
cards.upright_meaning: Page of Pentacles represents learning, skill, and practicality. It symbolizes patience and finance. This card encourages you to learn new skills, focus on practicality, stay patient, pay attention to finance, and take responsibility.
cards.reversed_keywords: Learning difficulties, lack of skill, impracticality, impatience
cards.reversed_meaning: When Page of Pentacles is reversed, it may indicate learning difficulties or lack of skill. You may be impractical or lack patience, needing to work harder to learn and develop your skills.
cards.symbolism: Page of Pentacles is typically depicted as a young person holding a pentacle, symbolizing learning and skill.

cards.name: Knight of Pentacles
cards.upright_keywords: Effort, responsibility, practicality, stability, patience, focus
cards.upright_meaning: Knight of Pentacles represents effort, responsibility, and practicality. It symbolizes stability and patience. This card encourages you to work hard, take responsibility, focus on practicality, maintain stability, stay patient, and stay focused.
cards.reversed_keywords: Insufficient effort, avoiding responsibility, impracticality, instability
cards.reversed_meaning: When Knight of Pentacles is reversed, it may indicate insufficient effort or avoiding responsibility. You may be impractical or unstable, needing to work harder and take responsibility.
cards.symbolism: Knight of Pentacles is typically depicted as a knight on horseback holding a pentacle, symbolizing effort and responsibility.

cards.name: Queen of Pentacles
cards.upright_keywords: Abundance, motherhood, practicality, security, creativity, generosity
cards.upright_meaning: Queen of Pentacles represents abundance, motherhood, and practicality. It symbolizes security and creativity. This card encourages you to enjoy abundance, show motherhood, focus on practicality, establish security, express creativity, and stay generous.
cards.reversed_keywords: Insufficient abundance, over-protectiveness, impracticality, lack of security
cards.reversed_meaning: When Queen of Pentacles is reversed, it may indicate insufficient abundance or over-protectiveness. You may be impractical or lack security, needing to balance your finances and emotions better.
cards.symbolism: Queen of Pentacles is typically depicted as a woman holding a pentacle, symbolizing abundance and motherhood.

cards.name: King of Pentacles
cards.upright_keywords: Wealth, success, leadership, practicality, stability, generosity
cards.upright_meaning: King of Pentacles represents wealth, success, and leadership. It symbolizes practicality and stability. This card encourages you to accumulate wealth, celebrate success, demonstrate leadership, focus on practicality, maintain stability, and stay generous.
cards.reversed_keywords: Wealth loss, limited success, lack of leadership, impracticality
cards.reversed_meaning: When King of Pentacles is reversed, it may indicate wealth loss or limited success. You may lack leadership or be impractical, needing to balance your finances and leadership responsibilities better.
cards.symbolism: King of Pentacles is typically depicted as a man holding a pentacle, symbolizing wealth and leadership.
